title: Speculative Decoding
description: A serving technique that uses a cheaper draft path to propose several next tokens, then asks the main model to verify them so decode-heavy requests can finish with fewer expensive turns.
openingSummary: Speculative decoding is a latency-reduction technique for autoregressive serving. Instead of waiting for the main model to produce one token at a time, the system lets a cheaper draft path guess several tokens, then has the stronger verifier model accept the matching prefix or reject the rest.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: Speculative decoding is a serving system around the same underlying model, not a new model architecture. Its job is to reduce reader-visible decode latency by pairing the main model with a cheaper draft model or draft path that proposes multiple next tokens before the main model checks them.

whereItSits.title: Where It Sits
whereItSits.body: It sits inside the inference-serving runtime alongside batching, key-value cache management, and request scheduling. The model weights still define the actual language model. Speculative decoding changes how the serving stack uses those weights during decode so one expensive verifier pass can accept more than one token.

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.body: The draft path predicts a short run of future tokens. The main model then evaluates that draft in parallel and keeps the longest prefix that matches its own next-token decisions. Accepted draft tokens let the system skip some slow one-token-at-a-time verifier turns. Rejected tokens still cost draft work plus verification, so the speedup depends on keeping acceptance high.

practicalImpact.title: Practical Impact
practicalImpact.body: This technique helps most on decode-heavy workloads where the main model is expensive and the draft path is much cheaper. It is less useful when acceptance is low, the verifier remains the main bottleneck, or the extra draft-model coordination adds memory, compute, or operational cost that outweighs the latency reduction.

related.title: Related To
related.body: Use the nearby serving pages below to trace how speculative decoding interacts with request batching, routing, inference-engine behavior, key-value cache reuse, and deployment choices.
